title: Symphyotrichum parviceps
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum parviceps is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Symphyotrichum, which includes several species of coneflowers native to North America. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Symphyotrichum parviceps is further classified as a member of the subfamily Asteroideae, which includes many species of flowering plants with daisy-like flowers. This plant is native to the southeastern United States and is typically found in dry to mesic environments.
uses: Symphyotrichum parviceps, also known as small white panicle aster, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
